refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To ensure atomicity and clarity, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refined FR focuses solely on the load phase, maintaining solution neutrality by specifying what must be achieved without prescribing how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times
Allocate sufficient power and torque resources to meet cycle time targets
Integrate load phase operations with upstream and downstream processes for smooth flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Integration complexity with other cycle phases
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on adaptive control which logically isolates the load phase timing optimization, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is reliable and timely
Control algorithms can respond quickly enough to influence cycle time
Operational policies allow adaptive control adjustments during load phase
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including feedback and control mechanisms in the FR to clarify the need for adaptive timing and support the selected DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for overall system control logic including adaptive cycle time control

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data for adaptive control

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated cycle time commands to execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core control function; minimal diffusion as it mainly interacts via telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Vehicle module is more about physical actuation; adaptive control logic would be diffused across vehicle subsystems.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and user commands, less relevant to adaptive cycle time control logic.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not cycle time control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Adaptive cycle time control is best contained within the NGHE Solution module as it centralizes control logic and can directly process telemetry input and issue tasking commands, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency.
If cycle time control logic grows complex, risk of coupling with other control functions within NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider decomposing adaptive cycle time control into a dedicated submodule within NGHE Solution to isolate complexity without spreading across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by adapting control based on real-time data, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of meeting p95 cycle time targets under operational conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with other control functions within the NGHE Solution module if complexity increases.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The adaptive cycle time control logic is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion and minimal diffusion as it centrally processes telemetry input and issues tasking commands. Other modules have low containment scores and higher diffusion, indicating appropriate ownership by NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measurement of cycle time p95 against the target under agreed duty cycles and site conditions, supported by telemetry data received via the defined interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear with the NGHE Solution module owning the adaptive control logic and interfacing appropriately with telemetry (input) and tasking (output) modules, ensuring traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the complexity growth of the adaptive cycle time control logic; if it increases, decompose it into a dedicated submodule within NGHE Solution to isolate complexity and avoid diffusion across modules.
Mitigate risks related to dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data by implementing robust data validation and fallback strategies within the NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: The current FR to DP to IM mapping is well aligned and passes all gates with clear ownership, containment, and verification feasibility. The adaptive cycle time control DP directly addresses the FR, and the NGHE Solution module is an appropriate IM with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the design is sufficiently detailed at this level to proceed with implementation and verification, while monitoring complexity growth as recommended. Further decomposition is not necessary at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth of the adaptive control logic and implement robust data validation and fallback strategies to mitigate telemetry dependency risks.

assumptions: Telemetry data will remain timely and accurate under operational conditions.
The NGHE Solution module can contain the adaptive control logic without excessive complexity.
open_risks: Potential coupling with other control functions if complexity increases.
Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
evidence_needed: Operational telemetry data quality and latency metrics.
Cycle time p95 measurement under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
